have.label: What they HAVE (tangible possessions and results)

before.state: A bag full of expensive balls they can't fully compress
before.prospect_language: I play ProV1 because I love the action around the green, and I find enough of them to never have to buy them. However, my launch monitor shows I lose 10 yards on drives.

before.state: Drives landing 20-50 yards shorter than 5-10 years ago
before.prospect_language: Me at 30 years old - Driver 280. 55 - Driver 240. 67 - Driver 210.
The new ProV balls are $55 a dozen

before.state: A closet full of half-tried ball brands from experimentation
before.prospect_language: I've been experimenting this year, using Bridgestone e12 & Callaway super soft. I'll use one brand for 8-10 rounds, then the other.

after.state: A ball engineered specifically for their swing speed — confirmed by robot testing
5-15 yards of distance they thought was gone forever
after.prospect_language: Two years ago, my average drive was about 205 yds. Now averaging 240.

after.state: $30/dozen performance that matches or beats $55 alternatives
One ball they trust — no more guessing
after.prospect_language: I buy 10 dozen logo balls in March every year and play them all season

experience.label: What they EXPERIENCE (daily reality on the course)

before.state: Watching playing partners outdrive them with seemingly less effort
before.prospect_language: I watch some people make a seemingly effortless swing and develop 110 mph clubhead speed. When I do what I think is the same motion, it's more like 95mph.

before.state: Hitting from one tee box farther up than they used to
before.prospect_language: I now land at the same spot from the blue tees as I did from the black tees years ago

before.state: The confusion of standing in the ball aisle with 30 options and no idea which is right
before.prospect_language: There are so many good golf balls to choose from these days

before.state: The quiet dread of watching another drive fall short
before.prospect_language: Now I crush one, check the distance, and it's 250

after.state: Stepping up to the tee knowing the ball is working FOR their swing, not against it
Seeing drives land past where they expected
after.prospect_language: I hit the longest drive I've ever hit on this hole

after.state: Walking into the pro shop or logging online and knowing exactly what to buy
after.prospect_language: I use the Vice Pro now, pretty much exclusively

after.state: Using the visual alignment system to line up putts and tee shots with confidence
after.prospect_language: [Latent — alignment technology is novel. Golfers currently draw lines with Sharpies or use Triple Track but no dedicated alignment system exists]

status.label: How OTHERS see them (social standing among playing partners)

before.state: The guy who can't keep up — playing from the forward tees, always the shortest drive in the group
before.prospect_language: It's not fair to play with a team as I just can't hold my own

before.state: The sucker paying $55/dozen for a ball he can't fully use
before.prospect_language: Stop wasting your money. You're not good enough to use prov1s

before.state: The guy always searching for a new ball — never settled, never satisfied

after.state: The smart golfer who plays the right ball for HIS game — not what marketing told him to play
after.prospect_language: I have really nice golf equipment without paying those ridiculous prices

after.state: The guy who gained yards when everyone else is losing them
The one his playing partners ask 'what ball are you playing?'
after.prospect_language: [Aspirational — this is the word-of-mouth moment the brand needs to engineer]

before.state: Frustrated — the body is betraying them and every round reminds them
before.prospect_language: My mind writes checks my body can't cash

before.state: Ripped off — the industry takes more of their money every year for the same ball
before.prospect_language: I'll be damned if I'll ever pay $3-5 for a premium golf ball

before.state: Uncertain — not confident they're playing the right ball but don't know how to find out
before.prospect_language: There are so many good golf balls to choose from these days

before.state: Resigned — distance loss feels permanent and irreversible
before.prospect_language: BIG drop between 50 and 60, and again by the time I hit 65

after.state: Confident — they KNOW why this ball works for their swing (data, not marketing)
after.prospect_language: I asked AI for a fitting and it put me into a Bridgestone B6. I immediately saw 10ish yards improvement

after.state: Relieved — off the premium ball treadmill, playing smarter not richer
after.prospect_language: Nearly the same performance, half the price

after.state: Hopeful — distance isn't just declining anymore, some of it came back
after.prospect_language: Two years ago, my average drive was about 205 yds. Now averaging 240.

after.state: Enjoyment — golf is fun again when the ball does what they expect
after.prospect_language: I love them. They perform really well.
